scope: Local prejoin lobby behavior when camera preview permission is denied before any LiveKit/Jitsi provider join.
expected: A NotAllowedError from createLocalVideoTrack surfaces a visible camera permission warning.
The lobby camera state degrades to off so the UI shows the avatar/off state instead of a blank enabled preview.
Microphone selection remains on and the user can still proceed with a no-camera lobby selection.
actualBefore: The hook surfaced the permission warning but left selection.cameraOn=true, so the pure lobby kept rendering the enabled video preview path even though no camera track existed.
fixApplied: On preview startup failure, useLobbyPreview now sets selection.cameraOn=false after setting the user-facing permission/camera error.
redEvidence: node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run apps/web/src/lib/call/use-lobby-preview.test.tsx -> failed: expected selection.cameraOn false, received true.
greenEvidence: node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run apps/web/src/lib/call/use-lobby-preview.test.tsx -> 1 file passed; 1 test passed.
node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run apps/web/src/lib/call/call-engine.test.tsx -> 1 file passed; 5 tests passed.
node_modules\.bin\tsc.CMD -p apps/web/tsconfig.json --pretty false -> passed with empty output.
limitations: This closes only the local device-denied/degraded lobby UI atom without external provider credentials.
It does not verify the real Chrome permission prompt, real device labels, external Jitsi page behavior, LiveKit media publishing, remote participant state, reconnect, or leave/rejoin traversal.
matrixImpact: Narrows RISK-MEDIA-LIVEKIT-JITSI-CALL for device denied/degraded prejoin UI without LiveKit/Jitsi credentials; the overall risk zone remains unverified.
